title: prunus × subhirtella f. autumnalis
intro: Prunus × subhirtella f. autumnalis, also known as the Autumn Cherry or Japanese Autumn Cherry, is a deciduous flowering tree that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its medicinal properties which include antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Prunus × subhirtella f. autumnalis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and support the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its bark, leaves, and flowers can be used individually or in combination to create medicinal remedies.
